remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
remarks.content: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI
remarks.contentTranslation: 我真心认为，如果你拿一个2025年的开源权重模型，为它搭建一个渗透测试框架，它就能完成这种沙箱逃逸，并在大多数网络中进行扫描/入侵。这之所以令人惊讶，仅仅是因为你假设了OpenAI。
remarks.translation: 引用托马斯·普塔塞克（Thomas Ptacek）的话
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: OpenAI’s accidental cyberattack against Hugging Face is science fiction that happened
remarks.content: This story is wild. The short version: OpenAI were running a cybersecurity test against an unreleased model, with the model's guardrail features turned off. Rather than solve the test, the model broke its way out of OpenAI's sandbox, then found exploits to break in to Hugging Face, all s
remarks.contentTranslation: 这个故事太疯狂了。简而言之：OpenAI 对一个未发布模型进行网络安全测试，并关闭了该模型的防护功能。结果模型没有解决测试问题，反而突破了 OpenAI 的沙盒，然后找到漏洞入侵了 Hugging Face，整个过程
remarks.translation: OpenAI对Hugging Face的意外网络攻击，是科幻照进现实。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
at some point in your engineering career, a wizened graybeard is going to lecture you about the importance of independently separable control plane vs data plane.

it is very important that you listen.

(and then try to learn about the management plane as early as you can..) https://t.co/EQZLBKcHof
remarks.translation: 在你的工程生涯中，总会有位饱经风霜的老前辈向你强调控制面与数据面独立分离的重要性。请务必认真倾听。（然后尽早去了解管理面……）https://t.co/EQZLBKcHof

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
If you were wondering how powerful AI is getting, Agents are now capable of escaping out of systems, finding their way to the internet, discovering zero day security vulnerabilities along the way, and then breaking into external systems - all in an attempt to complete their goal.

Ironically, the ultimate way we’re going to defend against these new risks is equally by throwing compute (in the form of AI) at our code bases, networks, and other systems. You’re going to want vastly more AI on the side of defense as you do on the side of offense. 

We’re entering a new era of what’s going to be possible with AI. Wild times ahead.
remarks.translation: 如果你好奇AI现在有多强大，智能体已经能够从系统中逃逸，自行找到通往互联网的路径，沿途发现零日安全漏洞，然后入侵外部系统——这一切都是为了完成它们的目标。讽刺的是，我们抵御这些新风险的终极方式，同样是将算力（以AI的形式）投入到代码库、网络和其他系统中。你需要在防御端部署的AI数量，远远超过攻击端。我们正迈入一个AI将带来全新可能性的时代。前方是狂野的时代。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
recorded Codex + ChatGPT Work + 10M user milestone pod with @akshaynathan_ who leads Productivity engineering. 

I’ve been on record that Work + GPT 5.6 is the most company defining launch since og chatgpt itself. this thing (with @arix’s computer use) is gonna reach >1B users worldwide.

thursday on @latentspacepod
remarks.translation: 录制了 Codex + ChatGPT Work + 10M 用户里程碑播客，嘉宾是负责生产力工程的 @akshaynathan_。我曾公开表示，Work + GPT 5.6 是自原始 ChatGPT 以来最具公司定义性的发布。这个产品（结合 @arix 的计算机使用功能）将在全球范围内达到 >1B 用户。周四在 @latentspacepod 播出。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
Teams don’t cohere by magic. Someone has to love the people and the outcome enough to metabolize conflict without abandoning either. 

The war against organizational entropy is actually just about encouraging healing and assuming good intent.
remarks.translation: 团队的凝聚力并非凭空而来。必须有人足够珍视成员与成果，在化解冲突时既不放弃任何一方，也不回避矛盾。对抗组织熵增的本质，不过是鼓励修复裂痕、相信善意。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
I've been dreaming about this ship for a while. Lots of tedious infra work behind the scenes that gave us beautiful results…

Up to ⏱︎ 30% faster deployments, ⚡︎ 60% better time-to-first-byte, ▽ less data transfer usage, and ⛁ wildly more efficient underlying storage! https://t.co/Ez8t6qqpN4
remarks.translation: 这艘船我梦想已久。幕后大量繁琐的基础设施工作，换来了令人惊艳的成果……部署速度提升⏱︎高达30%，首字节时间改善⚡︎60%，数据传输用量▽减少，底层存储⛁效率大幅提升！https://t.co/Ez8t6qqpN4
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text:
Okay this is wild: OpenAI agent during evaluation, escaped sandboxing and hacked into HuggingFace. 

Because OpenAI models don’t allow advanced cyber capabilities, HuggingFace used a Chinese open model to contain the rogue OpenAI agent.
remarks.translation: 这太疯狂了：OpenAI的智能体在评估过程中，突破了沙箱限制并入侵了HuggingFace。由于OpenAI模型不允许具备高级网络能力，HuggingFace使用了一个中国开源模型来遏制这个失控的OpenAI智能体。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
This is an important update from Substack.

I just plugged in some X posts from some creators who are prolific posters (like 5-10 posts per hour) in Pangram and what do you know...

LinkedIn has it the worst but I think @X should do something about this as well.

The sad truth is that spamming the feed with slop works if you want to get attention and go viral.

If you want respect on the other hand...
remarks.translation: 这是来自 Substack 的重要更新。我刚刚在 Pangram 中导入了一些高产创作者（比如每小时发 5-10 条帖子）的 X 平台帖子，结果你猜怎么着……LinkedIn 的情况最糟糕，但我认为 @X 也应该对此采取一些措施。可悲的事实是，如果你想吸引眼球并走红，用垃圾内容刷屏确实有效；但如果你想赢得尊重，那就另当别论了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Sam Altman

remarks.text: One of our engineers built an interactive math art generator using the new 3.6 Flash model. You can customize speed, colors, and geometry parameters on the fly, then export the design directly to a 3D-printable STL file. Watch the video to see how the physical print turned out! https://t.co/MVivWkv0Nk

remarks.name: Claude
remarks.role: Claude is an AI assistant built by anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8dz3D or download the app.
remarks.text:
New in Claude Cowork: teach Claude a skill.

Record your screen while you do a task, talk through it as you go, and Claude turns it into a skill it can run again. Find it under Record a skill in the + menu of the Claude desktop app. 

Available on Pro, Max, and Team plans. https://t.co/9OgJLOKWzj
remarks.translation: Claude Cowork 新功能：教 Claude 掌握一项技能。在执行任务时录制屏幕并同步讲解，Claude 会将其转化为可重复运行的技能。在 Claude 桌面应用的 + 菜单中找到"录制技能"即可使用。适用于 Pro、Max 和 Team 套餐。https://t.co/9OgJLOKWzj
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Josh Woodward
remarks.role: VP, Google GoogleLabs GeminiApp GoogleAIStudio
remarks.text:
Today’s launches are all about better performance, lower latency, and a smaller bill.

+ 3.6 Flash cuts token usage by up to 65% on complex coding
+ 3.5 Flash-Lite reaches speeds of 350 output tokens/sec

Both are live in the Gemini app today!

Next up: Gemini 3.5 Pro, which has officially entered partner testing.
remarks.translation: 今天的发布重点在于更优性能、更低延迟和更小账单。+ 3.6 Flash 在复杂编码任务中可减少高达65%的令牌使用量 + 3.5 Flash-Lite 输出速度达到每秒350个令牌 两者今日已在 Gemini 应用中上线！接下来：Gemini 3.5 Pro 已正式进入合作伙伴测试阶段。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

Want to fix SF? It’s time to reform the city charter and support Mayor Lurie’s efforts to do so

All the doom loop beneficiaries are going to work hard to lie to you about how bad charter reform is

Remember: Lurie’s charter reform will fix San Francisco https://t.co/DsgrEvL9pV

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
Seems like we went from OpenAI vs. Anthropic to geopolitics overnight.

Or maybe it was geopolitics all along.
remarks.translation: 看起来我们一夜之间就从OpenAI与Anthropic的竞争转向了地缘政治。又或许，这从来都是地缘政治。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
The more I’ve relied on my second brain, the dumber my main brain has become.

I’m realizing there’s tremendous value in carrying around a lot of facts, half-baked ideas, and loose threads in your head.

Your subconscious keeps cooking. It makes connections between ideas, extends them, and generates new ones. 

You also have much better real-time recall during conversations, which lets you connect ideas and think faster.

I believe there’s value in a second brain. figuring out how to use one without my main brain getting weaker.
remarks.translation: 我越是依赖第二大脑，主脑就变得越迟钝。我逐渐意识到，在脑海中储存大量事实、半成型的想法和未解的线索，其实有着巨大的价值。你的潜意识会持续运作，在想法之间建立联系，延伸它们，并催生新的灵感。在对话中，你也能更即时地调取信息，从而更快地连接思路、加速思考。我相信第二大脑有其价值，但关键在于如何运用它，而不让主脑因此退化。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: A Fireside Chat with Cat and Thariq from the Claude Code team
remarks.content: Earlier this month I hosted a fireside chat session at the AI Engineer World's Fair with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic's Claude Code team. We talked about Claude Code, Claude Tag, Fable, coding agent security, evals, tool design, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 本月初，我在AI工程师世界博览会主持了一场炉边谈话，与Anthropic公司Claude Code团队的Cat Wu和Thariq Shihipar进行了交流。我们讨论了Claude Code、Claude Tag、Fable、编码代理安全、评估、工具设计等话题。
remarks.translation: 与Claude Code团队的Cat和Thariq围炉夜话
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
Memory "loss" and compaction still feels like a huge issue with all harnesses. A tendency to forget and get confused is pretty frustrating for the end user.

The "interpretability" when these errors occur is also pretty poor.

Skills as a way to store this information feels like one of the root issues.

Would be cool to have some kind of format/language to make this better.
remarks.translation: 内存“丢失”和压缩问题在所有工具链中仍是一大痛点。系统容易遗忘和混淆，对最终用户而言相当令人沮丧。当这些错误发生时，“可解释性”也非常差。技能作为存储这些信息的方式，似乎是根本问题之一。如果能有一种格式或语言来改善这一点，那就太棒了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: Reverse-engineering is cheap now
remarks.content: I keep hearing anecdotes from people who used coding agents to reverse-engineer and automate devices in their homes. I think this is an interesting illustration of the impact of the reduced cost of writing code. Prior to agents, it was entirely possible to reverse-engineer home devi
remarks.contentTranslation: 我不断听到有人利用编程代理逆向工程并自动化家中设备的故事。我认为这生动地说明了编写代码成本降低所带来的影响。在代理出现之前，逆向工程家用设备是完全可行的，
remarks.translation: 逆向工程现在成本很低。

remarks.text: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
remarks.content: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good.</
remarks.contentTranslation: 在《用Rust重写Bun》一文中，Jared Sumner提出了以下观点：Claude Code v2.1.181（于6月17日发布）及后续版本使用了Bun的Rust移植版。启动速度在Linux上提升了10%，但除此之外几乎无人察觉。无趣才是好事。
remarks.translation: Claude Code 现在使用基于 Rust 编写的 Bun。

Google

remarks.text: Inkling: Our open-weights model
remarks.content: Inkling: Our open-weights model Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab just released their first open-weights model. Inkling is "a Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 975B total parameters, 41B active" - an Apac
remarks.contentTranslation: Inkling：我们的开源权重模型。Mira Murati的Thinking Machines Lab刚刚发布了他们的首个开源权重模型。Inkling是“一个总参数975B、活跃参数41B的混合专家Transformer”——来自Apac。
remarks.translation: Inkling：我们的开放权重模型
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.text: Quoting Linus Torvalds
remarks.content: I realize that some people really dislike AI, but this is an area where I'm willing to absolutely put my foot down as the top-level maintainer. Linux is not one of tho
remarks.contentTranslation: 我明白有些人非常不喜欢AI，但作为顶级维护者，在这个问题上我绝对寸步不让。Linux不属于那种
remarks.translation: 引用林纳斯·托瓦兹的话

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 为什么Frontier生态系统必须开放——Matei Zaharia与Reynold Xin，Databricks
podcasts.originalTitle: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
podcasts.excerpt: 我们非常高兴地欢迎 Databricks 加入 AIEWF，与 AI 工程师生态系统中数百家顶尖公司共襄盛举。LS 订阅用户可使用专属折扣，突破晚鸟票价限制，并获取价值超过 $50k 的赞助商福利！大家仍在热议 Satya 的《前沿生态系统》文章，但真正动手构建的人却寥寥无几（<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…
podcasts.originalExcerpt: We’re excited to have Databricks join us at AIEWF, among hundreds of the top companies in the AI Engineer ecosystem. LS subscribers can use their discount to get past the late bird pricing and access over $50k in sponsor offers! Everyone is still talking about Satya’s Frontier Ecosystems post, but few have actually built a (<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 构建一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律持续成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈对此深信不疑——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个公开猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到模型只是找到了一个反例时，他松了口气——这为他争取了又一两年时间，让他毕生投入的事业不至于那么快就被 AI 超越。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估工具的公司的创始人兼首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾视为人类专属任务的速度，有着独特的见解……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
